sahara.title: Atlas Mountains & Sahara
sahara.subtitle: Experience the majestic peaks and endless dunes.

ouarzazate.title: Ouarzazate
ouarzazate.desc: Known as the door of the desert, this city is famous for its cinematic landscapes and ancient kasbahs.

merzouga.title: Merzouga Dunes
merzouga.desc: Witness breathtaking sunsets over the towering sand dunes of Erg Chebbi. A truly magical experience.

ait_ben_haddou.title: Aït Benhaddou
ait_ben_haddou.desc: A historic fortified village along the former caravan route between the Sahara and Marrakech.

coastal.title: Coastal Escapes
coastal.subtitle: Discover tranquil beaches and vibrant coastal towns.

essaouira.title: Essaouira
essaouira.desc: A charming port city with a beautiful medina, historic ramparts, and a relaxed, artsy vibe.

taghazout.title: Taghazout
taghazout.desc: A famous fishing village turned surfing paradise. Perfect for catching waves and relaxing on pristine shores.

agadir.title: Agadir
agadir.desc: Known for its wide crescent beach, bustling promenade, and year-round sunshine.

city.title: City Exploring
city.subtitle: Immerse yourself in the bustling medinas and rich history.

marrakech.title: Marrakech
marrakech.desc: The Red City, filled with vibrant souks, stunning palaces, and the lively Jemaa el-Fnaa square.

chefchaouen.title: Chefchaouen
chefchaouen.desc: The famous Blue City nestled in the Rif Mountains, offering picturesque alleys and a calm atmosphere.

fes.title: Fes
fes.desc: Home to the oldest continuously functioning university in the world and an incredibly intricate medieval medina.

blog.title: Travel Guides & Moroccan Road Trips
blog.subtitle: Discover the beauty of Morocco with our exclusive travel guides.

ultimate-road-trip.title: The Ultimate Moroccan Road Trip: Tangier to Merzouga
ultimate-road-trip.excerpt: Experience the diversity of Morocco, from Mediterranean charm to golden dunes, in this comprehensive road trip guide.
contentBlocks: Starting your journey in Tangier, you'll immediately feel the blend of Mediterranean breeze and North African culture. The drive south takes you through the stunning Rif Mountains, where the blue city of Chefchaouen awaits.

As you continue towards the center of the country, the landscape transforms. The lush greenery gives way to the stark beauty of the Middle Atlas, passing through Ifrane and the cedar forests of Azrou.

The grand finale of this ultimate road trip is reaching the Sahara. Driving into Merzouga and seeing the golden dunes of Erg Chebbi rise from the flat desert is a memory that will last a lifetime. Prepare your SUV for an unforgettable adventure.

imperial-cities.title: Exploring the Imperial Cities: A Historic Drive
imperial-cities.excerpt: Take a journey through time by visiting Morocco's four imperial cities: Marrakech, Fes, Meknes, and Rabat.
contentBlocks: Morocco's rich history is best experienced by driving between its four Imperial Cities. Start in Rabat, the modern capital, where ancient ruins meet the Atlantic Ocean.

A short drive inland brings you to Meknes, the city of Moulay Ismail, featuring massive gates and the nearby Roman ruins of Volubilis. From there, the short journey to Fes takes you into the spiritual heart of the country.

Finally, head south to Marrakech, the Red City. The drive from Fes to Marrakech is long but incredibly scenic, offering views of the Atlas Mountains before arriving in the bustling, vibrant city of the south.

atlantic-escapade.title: The Atlantic Escapade: Casablanca to Agadir
atlantic-escapade.excerpt: Drive the scenic coastline of Morocco, enjoying fresh seafood, surfing spots, and relaxed coastal vibes.
contentBlocks: The Atlantic coast of Morocco offers a completely different driving experience. Starting in the economic hub of Casablanca, take the coastal road down towards the historic port of El Jadida.

The real gem of this trip is Essaouira. This windy, artistic city is perfect for a relaxed stopover. The drive is relatively flat and easy, making it ideal for a comfortable luxury car or an economic compact.
Continuing south, you'll reach the surfing paradise of Taghazout before finally arriving in Agadir. The coastal highway provides endless ocean views and spectacular sunsets throughout your journey.

driving-tips.title: Essential Tips for Driving in Morocco
driving-tips.excerpt: A complete tourist guide covering rules, navigation, and choosing the right car for the diverse Moroccan terrain.
contentBlocks: Driving in Morocco is generally safe and roads between major cities are well-maintained. Always carry your driver's license, passport, and rental documents. Speed limits are strictly enforced by police checkpoints.

Navigation is straightforward with apps like Google Maps, but downloading offline maps is highly recommended, especially when heading into the Atlas Mountains or the Sahara where signal can be spotty.

Choosing the right car is crucial. For coastal and city driving, an economic or luxury sedan is perfect. However, if you plan to conquer the Atlas or head to the desert, renting a reliable SUV is highly recommended for comfort and safety.

aboutPage.heroSubtitle: Experience Morocco with unparalleled luxury and comfort.
aboutPage.storyContent: Founded with a deep love for Moroccan landscapes, Drive Car Go began as a small vision to elevate the standard of travel. We quickly transitioned from a standard rental agency to a dedicated provider of premium travel experiences. Today, we pride ourselves on offering a fleet that meets the highest standards of luxury, paired with local expertise that ensures every journey is unforgettable.
